jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, AI Platform
jobs.description:
Ideal start timeline: September 2026

Role status: Exempt

Compensation: Our target hiring range is $180,000 - $220,000 plus participation in our Annual Bonus Program with eligibility for $12,000 bonus. Actual compensation will be commensurate with experience and skills.

Campminder’s Flexible Working Location:Our employees have the option to work 100% remotely within the United States or their choice of days at home and at our office in Boulder, Colorado. We host a variety of all-company hybrid meetings and social events. We require anybody working remotely to have a very reliable, high-speed internet connection.

We know the best people can choose to work anywhere.

Here’s a few reasons why 100+ of them choose Campminder:

  • With 20+ years experience of serving the industry through its digital transformation, we’re stable, profitable, and have developed a loyal customer base (that continues to grow).
  • We build software for summer camps, an industry that enables meaningful experiences for kids.
  • We work on interesting, ambitious projects that create real value for our clients.
  • We know our team members feel their work has an impact on the organization’s purpose.
  • At the same time, we are genuinely committed to work/life balance. Our team members feel they have the flexibility to take time off when needed and feel supported in making use of flexible working arrangements.
  • We invest in emerging technology and cutting-edge leadership and are proud to take an “AI-Enabled” approach in our solutions.
  • We’ve been listed on Outside Magazine’s 50 Best Places to Work for 8 consecutive years for our values-led culture and employee experience.

This role’s mission & overview:

Campminder is building an AI layer beneath every product we make, grounded on 25 years of camp data across close to 1,700 camps and a RAG system that’s live today. This role builds that shared foundation, the retrieval, evaluation, services, and patterns every product team builds on, and works across those teams to turn it into AI capabilities camp leaders actually use. The hard part isn’t producing a demo. It’s designing infrastructure many teams can build on, defining how its quality is measured, and getting it adopted in the wild. It’s not a role for adding an AI feature so a product looks current. It’s for someone who sees the whole system, finds the seam that matters, and makes the complexity smaller for everyone downstream.

As a Senior Product Manager of AI Platform on our Product team, you will:

  • Build and own the shared AI infrastructure our products stand on: retrieval and grounding on camp data (the RAG system is live, extend it), evaluation and observability, and the services and patterns every team reuses instead of rebuilding.
  • Work across product teams to turn that foundation into capabilities in the wild. Shape how it lands in each product, keep teams unblocked, and drive real adoption. This cross-functional work is most of the job.
  • Own how quality and trust are measured across the platform, precisely enough to catch a regression before a customer does, with a person in the loop where the stakes are highest.
  • Lead the lifecycle of LLM and ML work streams with your AI and data engineering partners, and make deliberate build vs. buy and train vs. fine-tune vs. ground-with-RAG calls without chasing hype.
  • Prototype hands-on to explore and de-risk direction, and expect to throw a healthy share of it away. It’s one tool for finding the right thing to build, not the whole job.
  • Grow the internal-automation side as the foundation matures, and write down what you and your partners learn so the next team starts ahead.

We think a successful candidate will bring:

  • Systems thinking, pattern recognition, and a bias to simplify. You see how the whole thing fits, spot the pattern that repeats, and reach for the simplest design that works. This is the core.
  • AI product depth. You’ve productized AI before, not just added an AI feature. You reason well about when to train or fine-tune versus ground with retrieval, you know RAG and evaluation in practice, and you hold your own with AI engineers on models, agents, and trade-offs. This role builds the AI infrastructure itself, so this depth is required, not a bonus. You prototype hands-on to de-risk direction, and you don’t need to own the MLOps infrastructure yourself.
  • A platform and infrastructure mindset. You’ve built foundations other teams build on, you treat those teams as your customers, and you keep them unblocked without building everything they ask for.
  • Cross-functional influence. You get things adopted across teams you don’t own, earning trust and driving real usage rather than shipping and hoping. This is where most of the value lands.
  • A track record of shipping through the full loop, from idea to working build to production to iterated. You can point to what you built, what got used, and what you changed. Talking about iteration isn’t the same as living it, and we’ll know the difference.
  • An eval instinct. You ask “how would we know this is good?” before “how do we build it?”, and you can define quality precisely enough to measure and defend it.
  • Coachability. You get sharper when someone pushes back, not defensive, and you often set the pace rather than slow it.

Our Interview Process:

  1. 45 min - interview with People & Culture
  2. 60 min - interview with Hiring Manager
  3. Take-home assignment on your own time: a real Campminder AI problem. We’re looking at how you define “good,” how you’d evaluate it, and how you reason about a system that won’t behave the same way twice.
  4. 60 min - present and defend your assignment to a technical and product panel. Expect pushback. How you handle it matters as much as the answer.

A few of the benefits we are proud to offer:

  • Robust medical, dental, and vision coverage options with generous employer contributions, plus a $500 employer HSA contribution for HSA-compatible plans
  • Ability to choose where you work - remotely, in the office, or a mix!
  • A variety of resources to support mental health and emotional well-being
  • 12 weeks of 100% paid parental leave for all new parents, including via adoption, surrogacy, and foster care
  • 401(k) with 4% company matching
  • Trust-Based (flexible) PTO (and yes, we use it!)
  • $900/year wellness allowance
  • Company-paid subscriptions, training, and support for using AI professionally and personally. We have a team dedicated to enabling our AI capabilities for our team members and our customers!

We encourage people of all backgrounds to apply:

We’re actively taking steps to make sure our culture is inclusive and that our processes and practices promote equity for all, including people of color, people from working-class backgrounds, women, and members of the LGBTQ+ community. We welcome and encourage applications from people with these identities or members of other historically marginalized groups.

Research shows that women and people of color tend not to apply to jobs unless they believe they are 100% qualified and apply to fewer senior-level positions. With that in mind, we encourage you to apply if you’re not sure whether you meet our qualifications. We’d love to have the opportunity to consider you!

We encourage applications from parents, parents-to-be, and those responsible for the caretaking of others. We offer paid parental leave for birthing and non-birthing parents (including for adoption, surrogacy, and foster care placement) and paid loss leave to recover from miscarriage or stillbirth. The company’s HSA and wellness allowance contributions may be used toward childcare, eldercare, adoption fees, and fertility treatments like IVF, among other expenses.

jobs.title: Account Coordinator, Paid Social
jobs.description:
WHY DEPT®?

We are a Growth Invention company built to help the world’s most ambitious brands grow faster. Operating at the intersection of technology and marketing, we create what is next by pioneering ideas, acting fast, and moving further because standing still just is not in our DNA.

We are drawn to people who stay curious, move with intent, and never stop inventing. Our culture runs on three values: better together, relentlessly curious, and get sh*t done. It is how we work, how we grow, and how we make things that matter.

At DEPT®, you will find the freedom to explore, the space to collaborate, and the trust to make a real impact for our clients, for each other, and for the world we are helping to build.

THE ROLE:

As a Account Coordinator, Paid Social, you will contribute to assigned paid social media campaigns that exceed client expectations.

Reports to: Senior Manager, Paid Social

WHAT YOU’LL DO:

  • Implement Paid Social campaigns by assembling plans and objectives, creating schedules, organizing, reporting data, and tracking progress
  • Collect, analyze, and process large amounts of data
  • Manage your workflow to ensure timely delivery on projects
  • Craft clear and compelling internal and external communications
  • Work collaboratively and cross-functionally across internal channels to meet the needs of the team and clients
  • Maintain a working knowledge of Meta Ads
  • Prepare weekly/monthly reports and make recommendations for optimization
  • Own weekly campaign health checks / pacing updates

WHAT YOU BRING:

  • A minimum of 6 months’ experience with Meta ads
  • Desire to quickly learn and master social channels
  • Proven prior work experience in a production-oriented or similar role
  • Strong analytical and reporting skills and be proficient in Google Sheets (or Excel); you already know vlookup, pivot tables, concatenating, text to columns, and love working with numbers
  • High level of independence, curiosity and desire to grow your responsibilities and skill set

Additional things that will impress us:

  • You are an energetic; eager to roll up your sleeves and be hands on, learn, and grow in the paid social media space
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • You are entrepreneurial spirited and a self-starter; able to work both independently and in a team environment
  • A desire to stay current with the latest marketing trends

WHAT DO WE OFFER?

  • Healthcare, Dental, and Vision coverage
  • 401K plan, plus matching
  • PTO
  • Paid Company Holidays
  • Parental Leave

WE SUPPORT YOU BEING YOU:

DEPT® is an equal opportunity employer (EOE). We believe our work is its best when our people feel safe and free to be themselves. We’re committed to an inclusive and barrier-free recruitment and selection process and workplace, regardless of anyone’s identity, background, beliefs, lifestyle, or disabilities. So tell us about any accommodations you need. It’s totally confidential and only used to make sure you feel fully supported at every step.

DEPT® participates in E-Verify, meaning your Form I-9 information will be sent to the federal government to confirm you are authorized to work in the U.S.

We are a B Corp-certified company passionate about purpose-driven work.  Our hope is that you can feel good about the contributions DEPT® is making to the world and we always have an open door for your ideas in making the world a better place.

DEPT® is a Growth Invention company built to help the world’s most ambitious brands grow faster. Operating at the intersection of technology and marketing, our 4,000+ specialists deliver growth invention across Brand & Media, Experience, Commerce, CRM, and Technology & Data. We’re 50|50 tech and marketing, partner-led, and first to move. Clients include Google, Lufthansa, Coach, eBay, and OpenAI.

Learn more about DEPT®

DIVERSITY, EQUITY, & INCLUSION

At DEPT®, we take pride in creating an inclusive workplace where everyone has an equal opportunity to thrive. We actively seek to recruit, develop, nurture, and retain talented individuals from diverse backgrounds, with varying skills and perspectives.

Not sure you meet all qualifications? Apply, and let us decide! Research shows that women and members of underrepresented groups tend not to apply for jobs when they think they may not meet every requirement, when in fact they do. We believe in giving everyone a fair chance to shine.

We also encourage you to reach out to us and discuss any reasonable adjustments we can make to support you throughout the recruitment process and your time with us.

#LI-remote

jobs.description:
WHY DEPT®?

We are pioneers at heart. What this means, is that we are always leaning forward, thinking of what we can create tomorrow that does not exist today. Meaning, that with people across 20+ countries, we’re big enough to provide you with the best tools, global opportunities, and benefits that help you thrive. While acting small by investing in you, your growth, your team, and giving you the autonomy to solve our clients’ problems, no matter where you are in the world.

ABOUT YOU

As an Account Manager, Programmatic, you will support the execution and optimization of programmatic campaigns within our retail vertical. You’ll work closely with Account Leads, Coordinators, and cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality work, drive performance, and ensure strong client satisfaction. You bring a mix of strategic thinking, operational excellence, and a proactive, solutions-oriented mindset.

THE ROLE:

Our Account Coordinators are analytical, detail-oriented, and they understand the “Why” when it comes to activation strategy, budgeting, bidding, and testing methodology. As an Account Coordinator, you will have a good understanding of Paid Search and proficiency in Excel/Sheets. You will own activation strategy, campaign management, and communication of specific portions of client accounts. Our Account Coordinators are capable communicators and vital members of our large account teams at DEPT®!

While DEPT® is a remote-first company, this role requires candidates to be located in either the Mountain or Pacific time zones.

  • Developing a strong proficiency in core paid media channel execution within Google ADs,SA360 etc.
  • Determining the best methodology for a campaign build: overall theme, keywords, audiences, parameters, settings, ads, etc. to best hit client goals.
  • Monitoring campaigns daily to look for performance fluctuations and trends.
  • Developing client-facing performance write-ups for the Account Manager’s review.
  • Crafting clear and compelling internal and external communications.
  • Understanding different bidding methodologies (smart bidding vs manual) and the strategy behind the bid adjustments as well as owning the implementation from standard bids to other modifiers as needed.
  • Understanding key concepts around audience strategy (remarketing, audience targeting/layering, persona development).
  • Working collaboratively and cross-functionally across internal channels to manage project deliverables and ensure client satisfaction

  • Proven prior work experience in a production-oriented or similar role
  • Strong analytical and reporting skills and be proficient in Excel; you already know vlookup, pivot tables, concatenating, text to columns, and love working with numbers
  •  While DEPT® is a remote-first company, this role requires candidates to be located in either the Mountain or Pacific time zones.

Additional things that will impress us:

  • A Google Ads certification
  • You are an energetic and creative search marketing enthusiast; eager to roll up your sleeves and be hands on, learn, and grow in the search engine marketing space
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • You are entrepreneurial spirited and a self-starter; able to work both independently and in a team environment
  • A desire to stay current with the latest marketing trends

So tell us about any accommodations you need. It’s totally confidential and only used to make sure you feel fully supported at every step.

DEPT® participates in E-Verify, meaning your Form I-9 information will be sent to the federal government to confirm you are authorized to work in the U.S.

We are a B Corp-certified company passionate about purpose-driven work.  Our hope is that you can feel good about the contributions DEPT® is making to the world and we always have an open door for your ideas in making the world a better place.

DEPT® is a Growth Invention company built to help the world’s most ambitious brands grow faster. Operating at the intersection of technology and marketing, our 4,000+ specialists deliver growth invention across Brand & Media, Experience, Commerce, CRM, and Technology & Data. We’re 50|50 tech and marketing, partner-led, and first to move. Clients include Google, Lufthansa, Coach, eBay, and OpenAI.

Learn more about DEPT®

DIVERSITY, EQUITY, & INCLUSION

At DEPT®, we take pride in creating an inclusive workplace where everyone has an equal opportunity to thrive. We actively seek to recruit, develop, nurture, and retain talented individuals from diverse backgrounds, with varying skills and perspectives.

Not sure you meet all qualifications? Apply, and let us decide! Research shows that women and members of underrepresented groups tend not to apply for jobs when they think they may not meet every requirement, when in fact they do. We believe in giving everyone a fair chance to shine.

We also encourage you to reach out to us and discuss any reasonable adjustments we can make to support you throughout the recruitment process and your time with us.

Want to know more about our dedication to diversity, equity, and inclusion? Check out our efforts here.

#LI-remote

The anticipated salary range for this position is listed below. Salary is based on a range of factors that include relevant experience, expertise, physical location, and organizational impact. This is based on a 40-hour workweek and leveling. Keep in mind, we’re flexible.

US Remote Range

$50,000—$60,000 USD

jobs.title: Lead Deployment Engineer - Public Sector - Federal
jobs.description:
Celonis is the global leader in Process Intelligence and the pioneer of Process Mining technology. As one of the world’s fastest-growing enterprise SaaS companies, we are changemakers pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. We invest heavily in advanced AI capabilities—specifically our Process Intelligence Graph—to turn data insights into immediate business action. We believe there is a massive opportunity to unlock global productivity and sustainability by placing intelligence at the core of every business process. Join our mission to make processes work for people, companies, and the planet.

The Team

Celonis Services is the delivery engine of our Celonis Customer Organization, responsible for turning the Celonis platform into measurable, business-critical outcomes for our customers. In the AI era, our role has elevated: as AI agents like our own Apollo take on more of the technical build, our people focus on the work that drives real impact—understanding our customers’ business-critical problems, designing the solutions that solve them, and quantifying the value delivered. Our Deployment Engineers and Deployment Architects pair deep domain and industry expertise with serious platform mastery and AI-augmented delivery, directing agentic systems while owning the judgment, architecture, and customer relationships that AI cannot replace. We deliver faster and smarter than ever, and we own the outcome: solution go-live, solution adoption, initial value realization support and the long-term success of our customers.

The Role

As a Lead Deployment Engineer, you are the backbone of Celonis delivery. You lead the hands-on implementation of Celonis solutions for enterprise customers, combining platform expertise with AI-augmented workflows to deliver high-quality outcomes, fast. You use Celonis’s AI deployment tools as your primary instruments - directing agents to build data models, generate analyses, and configure operational solutions, while applying your own judgment to validate outputs, adapt to customer context, and ensure business relevance.

This is not a traditional consulting role where you build everything manually. You are an AI-augmented delivery professional: you specify what needs to be built, direct AI agents to execute wherever possible, validate the results, and iterate with the customer. You generate value by understanding the customer’s business, translating their needs into technical specifications and solutions, and ensuring the platform delivers measurable impact.

The Work You’ll Do

Initiate and Connect

  • Conduct business discovery workshops to translate customers’ strategic objectives into high-impact Celonis use cases
  • Collaborate with the customer’s IT and business stakeholders to define project scope, data requirements, and success criteria
  • Ensure data connectivity and extraction pipelines are properly configured, working with specialist resources if required

Build & Value

  • Direct AI deployment agents (Apollo) to build data models, process analyses, KPIs, and dashboards - writing clear specifications and validating agent outputs against business requirements
  • Build and configure Celonis solutions across the platform: Process Intelligence Graph, data transformations, process analysis, operational solutions, and AI-powered insights
  • Identify process inefficiencies and root causes using Celonis technology, always in close collaboration with the customer
  • Validate AI-generated outputs for accuracy, business relevance, and alignment with customer context - applying domain knowledge where the AI cannot
  • Lead value creation workshops and define measures to improve process inefficiencies
  • Define and build KPIs measuring the customer’s success and quantify business impact
  • Design and build operational solutions to drive value, adoption and improve process conformance

Rollout, Adoption, and Handover

  • Enable key customer users to use and extend the solution independently, setting the foundation for long-term adoption
  • Manage project outcomes against timelines, scope, and customer expectations
  • Train and demonstrate to customer users, guiding them on the path to sustained value
  • Document solution design decisions and configurations for future reference and handoff

Domain & Industry Depth

  • Develop and maintain deep expertise in one or more process domains (e.g., Supply Chain, Order to Cash, Procure to Pay, IT Service Management) or industry verticals
  • Translate domain knowledge into scalable solution patterns, reference architectures, and best practices that the broader team can leverage
  • Proactively identify and standardize high-value use cases within your domain, collaborating with Product and Business Apps teams to shape the platform roadmap

What Makes This Role Different in the AI Era

  • You work with AI agents as your primary tools - your job is to direct, validate, and refine their output, not to write every line of code or build every analysis manually
  • You need strong specification and context engineering skills - the ability to translate a customer’s ambiguous business need into a precise instruction that an AI agent can execute correctly
  • You are expected to continuously learn and adapt as AI capabilities evolve, experimenting with new approaches and sharing what works with your peers
  • You balance speed with quality - AI accelerates execution, but you own the judgment calls on whether the output is right for this customer
  • You think in sustainable software solutions. While AI agents will generate first results quickly, you need to ensure that the operational solutions you build in Celonis are robust, user friendly, maintainable, and scalable

The Qualifications You Need

  • Experience: 7+ years in Management or IT consulting, data/solution consulting, or process improvement consulting within software vendors, consulting firms, or system integrators.
  • Platform skills: Ability to build and configure solutions on the Celonis platform, including data modeling, process analysis, KPI definition, and automation. Proficiency in PQL, SQL, and Python.
  • AI fluency: Comfort working with AI-assisted development tools (e.g., Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.). Ability to write clear specifications, evaluate AI-generated outputs, and iterate effectively. Experience with prompt engineering or context engineering is a strong plus.
  • Business and process domain expertise : Strong understanding /deep expertise (based on seniority level) of enterprise business processes and financial metrics. Knowledge of standard ERPs (SAP, Oracle) and process areas (Inventory Management, Order to Cash, Procure to Pay, Accounts Payable) is a strong plus.
  • Consulting skills: Ability to steer customer workshops, present findings to management, manage stakeholder expectations, and deliver outcomes in the face of adversity and ambiguity. Comfortable working with both technical and business audiences.
  • Analytical mindset: Excellent problem-solving skills with a value-driven orientation. Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions and quantify business impact.
  • Learning agility: Willingness to continuously develop expertise through experimentation. Comfort with ambiguity and evolving tools.
  • Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s in Technology Management, Business Informatics, Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or a comparable field.
  • Languages: Fluency in English required. Additional languages are a plus.
  • Travel: Willingness to travel to customer location up to 50%, according to customer requirement and project delivery phases.

The base salary range below is for the role in the specified location, based on a Full Time Schedule.

Total compensation package will include base salary + bonus/commission + equity + benefits (health, dental, life, 401k, and paid time off). Please note that the base salary range is a guideline, and that the actual total compensation offer will be determined based on various factors, including, but not limited to, applicant’s qualifications, skills, experiences, and location.

The base salary range below is for the role in Washington, D.C. based on a Full Time Schedule.

$162,000—$191,000 USD

What Celonis can offer you:

  • Pioneer Innovation: Work with the global leader in Process Mining and the Process Intelligence Graph to shape the future of AI-driven business operations.
  • Ownership from Day 1: Every full-time “Celonaut” is an owner, receiving Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) and merit-based refresh grants.
  • Unrivaled Family Support: Benefit from our inclusive parental leave policy—24 weeks of fully paid leave for primary carers and 12 weeks for supporting carers, available from your first day of employment.
  • Work-Life Integration: Enjoy Unlimited PTO (in applicable regions) and generous PTO globally, as well as a flexible hybrid work model that balances remote focus with vibrant office collaboration.
  • Continuous Growth: Elevate your skills through our 70-20-10 learning framework, mentorship programs, and access to a dedicated learning platform.
  • Holistic Well-being: Prioritize your health with subsidized Wellhub memberships, mental health counseling, and dedicated “Wellness Weeks” that prioritize work/life balance.
  • Drive Sustainability: Participate in annual Impact Days, where you receive paid time off to volunteer for community and environmental causes with your local office, or virtually.
  • Global Inclusion & Belonging: Find community through our Inclusion Think Tank and participate in our annual Inclusion Days, ensuring every voice is heard and valued.
  • Value-Driven Impact: Join a mission-led organization where our core values—Live for Customer Value, The Best Team Wins, We Own It, and Earth Is Our Future—drive every decision.

About Us:

Celonis makes processes work — for people, companies, and the planet. Powered by process mining and AI, the Celonis Process Intelligence Platform integrates process data and business context to create a living digital twin of business operations. We enable thousands of companies worldwide to understand how their business actually runs and, together with their partners, build intelligent solutions that transform and continuously improve the way they operate — unlocking billions in value. Celonis is headquartered in Munich, Germany, and New York City, USA, with more than 20 offices worldwide.

Get familiar with the Celonis Process Intelligence Platform by watching this video.

Celonis Inclusion Statement:

At Celonis, we believe our people make us who we are and that “The Best Team Wins”. We know that the best teams are made up of people who bring different perspectives to the table. And when everyone feels included, able to speak up and knows their voice is heard - that’s when creativity and innovation happen.

Your Privacy:

Any information you submit to Celonis as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Celonis’ Accessibility and Candidate Notices

By submitting this application, you confirm that you agree to the storing and processing of your personal data by Celonis as described in our Privacy Notice for the Application and Hiring Process.

Please be aware of common job offer scams, impersonators and frauds. Learn more here.

jobs.title: Influencer Campaign Manager
jobs.description:
Who We Are:

BrandCycle is an influencer and affiliate marketing platform representing over 7,000 social media influencers, bloggers, and content creators. Our members have a combined following of more than 300 million on social media. Most of our influencers have been creating content to drive sales for years. They are savvy shoppers who cross-promote and achieve high ROI for brands. We work with over 700 top brands, including Target, lululemon, Michael Kors, Under Armour, Crocs, Kate Spade, and Converse. In 2021, BrandCycle was acquired by StackCommerce, a leader in commerce content for major digital media publishers. StackCommerce and BrandCycle are backed by TPG, a top private equity firm that is known for its growth equity and impact investing.

The Role:

We’re hiring an Influencer Campaign Manager to own the strategy and execution of high-impact brand campaigns from first pitch through final report. You’ll be the connective tissue between brands and creators — translating client objectives into campaign strategy, partnering with the right creators to hit campaign and brand goals, and running the details that make campaigns land: outreach, negotiation, contracting, compliance, and reporting.

This is a great fit for someone who’s equally comfortable building a campaign strategy deck and chasing down a creator’s missing deliverable — and who wants to do both at a fast-growing company at a pivotal moment in its growth. This is a 100% remote position and must be based in the U.S.

What You Will Do:

  • End-to-end campaign management: Develop and execute influencer campaigns from strategy through launch. Own creator outreach, negotiation, contracting, and FTC compliance, through reporting and renewal strategy.
  • Strategic planning: Own the thinking behind each campaign — objectives, audiences, KPIs, budgets, and platform strategy that ladder directly to client business goals.
  • Performance monitoring and optimization: Track key metrics, analyze campaign effectiveness, and refine strategies based on what the data shows.
  • Industry and consumer insights: Stay current on market trends, the competitive landscape, and consumer behavior, and bring that thinking into campaign strategy.
  • Client leadership: Act as the strategic bridge between brands and creators, leading client communications with insight and recommendations.
  • Ownership of results: Take ownership of the numbers that define success, including campaign ROAS and renewal rate.
  • Cross-functional collaboration: Partner with Brand, Creator, Product, and Operations teams so creator needs are reflected in what we build and how we operate.

What We’re Looking For:

  • 4–6 years of experience in influencer marketing, campaign management, account management, or creator/partnership roles.
  • A proven track record running campaigns end-to-end and growing high-profile brand or creator relationships.
  • Working knowledge of affiliate networks, creator platforms, and social platforms.
  • Familiarity with AI and automation tools, and a genuine desire to expand how you use them.
  • Strong written, verbal, and presentation skills; you can influence and guide creators and lead client conversations and creator negotiations with confidence.
  • Experience with creator campaign tools like CreatorIQ, Aspire, or Sprout Social, and project management tools like Airtable, Asana, or Monday, is a plus.

Who You Are:

Relationship-driven

  • Warm and relationship-driven; you are a person creators and brands want to hear from.
  • Builds trust quickly and anticipates what partners need before they ask.
  • Balances fierce advocacy for the creator with what’s right for the business.

Self-directed

  • Entrepreneurial and eager to grow within the role and the company.
  • An effective communicator in a remote environment, comfortable working independently with minimal supervision.
  • Thrives in a fast-paced startup and enjoys wearing many hats.
  • Brings mature, measured judgment and solves problems independently.

Goals-driven

  • Deadline-driven and composed under pressure.
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail.
  • Analytical, and energized by reaching and exceeding revenue goals.
  • Fluent in KPIs and the business metrics that measure success.
  • Always spotting new opportunities and new brands to connect with.

A fast learner

  • Genuinely interested in digital marketing and e-commerce, and up to speed on the latest trends.
  • Comfortable with, and excited by new and emerging technology and media channels.

Compensation:

Our overall compensation and rewards package includes base pay, bonus or commission, and a variety of benefits. Base pay is determined within a range that allows you the opportunity to progress as you grow and develop within a role.  The anticipated annualized salary range for this role is $80,000 - $100,000 (this range may be modified in the future based on changing market conditions).  Your initial base pay will be determined based on several factors, including demonstrated skills, relevant education or training, experience, qualifications, travel requirements, and job location.

Benefits + Perks:

As a company, we have a lot to offer for the right candidate:

  • Health Benefits: We offer medical, dental, vision, life, and short-term and long-term disability insurance.
  • 401K: Eligible on your first day of employment.
  • Super Flex Time: Run errands as needed, just get your stuff done.
  • Unlimited Vacation: Take time to unwind with unlimited PTO, plus an office closure between Christmas and New Year’s.
  • Generous Parental Leave Program: 12 weeks of paid pregnancy disability and baby bonding leave, as well as 4 weeks of paid paternity leave.
  • WFH Office: Home office stipend and internet dollars to set you up for success.
  • Events: Happy hours, team bonding budgets and culture events (even while remote!) and an annual company offsite

BrandCycle/StackCommerce values a diverse workplace and strongly encourages people of color, LGBTQIA+, GNC, and NB individuals, people with disabilities, members of ethnic minorities, foreign-born residents, and veterans to apply. We respect and seek to empower each individual and support diverse cultures, perspectives, skills, and experiences within our workforce.

BrandCycle/StackCommerce is an equal opportunity employer. Applicants will not be discriminated against because of race, color, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, religion, national origin, citizenship status, disability, ancestry, marital status, veteran status, medical condition, or any protected category prohibited by local, state or federal laws.

jobs.title: VP, People
jobs.description:
About Nanit:

Nanit is moving families beyond overwhelm by transforming how parents understand their baby’s early years. We combine advanced AI, real-world data from billions of sleep hours, and clinical expertise to deliver the clarity and confidence every parent deserves. Trusted by over one million families and backed by $125M in funding, we’re building an entirely new category: predictive parenting intelligence.

Here, your work directly impacts millions of families. Our team of scientists, engineers, and parents is small but mighty - we share a mission to make early childhood easier, healthier, and more connected. If you want to work on technology that genuinely matters, join us.

About the Role:

We are seeking a strategic and people-first VP, People to serve as a key member of our leadership team and own the full people function globally. This role is designed for an experienced, tech-savvy HR leader who brings deep expertise in building and scaling people programs at high-growth tech companies, with a proven track record leading global teams across multiple geographies. The ideal candidate brings both strategic vision and hands-on execution ability, and who can shape and champion a distinctive company culture, serve as a trusted HRBP to senior leaders, design effective organizational structures, and architect competitive compensation and benefits programs. This leader will partner closely with the Chief Legal & Administrative Officer and executive team to drive people strategy, org design, and workforce planning that supports our global business objectives while fostering a high-performing, people-first culture.

What You’ll Be Doing:

  • Own and drive Nanit’s global people strategy, serving as a strategic partner to the Chief Legal & Administrative Officer and executive leadership team on all matters related to talent, culture, and organizational effectiveness
  • Design and scale global people programs that drive employee engagement, recognition, retention, and a high-performance culture across all geographies
  •  Build and continuously improve the full employee lifecycle experience – onboarding, development, performance, and offboarding
  • Lead organizational design and workforce planning efforts that enable Nanit to scale effectively, including defining team structures, reporting lines, spans of control, and headcount planning across the global organization
  • Build and own Nanit’s global compensation and benefits philosophy and framework, including market benchmarking, salary bands, incentive structures, and total rewards programs; administer equity and option plans
  • Serve as the senior employee relations leader, providing guidance and HR leadership across all employee matters globally, and coaching managers on performance, career development, and feedback
  • Oversee all HR operations and compliance globally, including HRIS, PEO management, payroll, benefits administration, and policy management across all markets where Nanit operates
  •  Lead the annual performance management and compensation planning cycle, ensuring rigor, fairness, and alignment to business priorities across the global organization
  • Lead and develop the global talent acquisition function to attract, hire, and retain exceptional talent, with a focus on building diverse, high-performing teams and delivering an outstanding candidate experience
  • Champion Nanit’s culture and values by acting as a trusted HRBP to senior leaders –   providing coaching, facilitating difficult conversations, and ensuring that people decisions reflect our commitment to an inclusive, high-trust workplace where every employee can do their best work

Who You Are:

  • 12+ years of progressive HR leadership experience, ideally with at least 3 years in a senior HR leadership role at a high-growth tech company
  • Proven experience leading and managing global teams across multiple countries and time zones, with a strong understanding of international employment practices
  • Deep expertise in organizational design, including structuring teams for scale, defining roles, and leading workforce planning in a fast-changing environment
  • Strong expertise in compensation and benefits design, including building salary bands, managing total rewards programs, and benchmarking against market data; experience administering equity and option plans is a plus
  • Solid knowledge of HR best practices, employment law, and people operations; experience managing HRIS, PEO, payroll, and benefits platforms
  • Exceptional relationship building skills; proven ability to effectively influence and partner with leaders across every level of the organization
  • Excellent communication skills. Must be able to effectively and succinctly communicate in writing and orally to executive management
  • High emotional intelligence, balances decision making with empathy and discretion
  • Strong operational leader that balances strategic thinking with hands-on execution in a fast-paced environment
  • Ability to perform with high pressure and short timelines

Why You’ll Love Working Here:

  • Hybrid in office schedule
  • Remote work from home month in August
  • Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need)
  • Equity options so you can share in our growth
  • Paid parental leave for all new parents
  • Employee discounts on Nanit products
  • Work from home stipend
  • Monthly team events

EEO, Salary and Location:

This is a hybrid role that will require prospective candidates to be in our NYC 3 days per week

Salary Range: $220,000 to $270,000 + equity, benefits and unlimited PTO. Nanit’s total compensation package includes access to healthcare benefits, a 401(k) plan, short-term and long-term disability coverage, and basic life insurance. Ultimately, in determining your pay, we’ll consider your location, experience, and other job-related factors.

We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected class.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Growth & Monetization
jobs.description:
About Nanit:

Nanit is moving families beyond overwhelm by transforming how parents understand their baby’s early years. We combine advanced AI, real-world data from billions of sleep hours, and clinical expertise to deliver the clarity and confidence every parent deserves. Trusted by over one million families and backed by $125M in funding, we’re building an entirely new category: predictive parenting intelligence.

Here, your work directly impacts millions of families. Our team of scientists, engineers, and parents is small but mighty - we share a mission to make early childhood easier, healthier, and more connected. If you want to work on technology that genuinely matters, join us.

About the Role:

We are looking for a growth-minded Senior Product Manager to drive impact across our full revenue funnel. This role will consider and evolve how customers discover, evaluate, purchase, and subscribe to and recommend products, from the moment they land on nanit.com through hardware checkout, software subscription, retention and referral.

This is a high-impact, full-funnel role for a strategic Product Manager who can identify and prioritize high-leverage opportunities across ecommerce and subscription monetization. You’ll partner closely with cross functional teams to align roadmap priorities with business goals. This role reports to the Senior Director of Product.

What You’ll Do:

Ecommerce & DTC Storefront

  • In partnership with the Sr. Director of Growth, manage the end-to-end DTC shopping experience on nanit.com
  • Drive e-commerce performance through funnel optimization, A/B testing, UX improvements, and Shopify platform management
  • Manage the Shopify platform and third-party integrations, identifying new opportunities to enhance customer experience and business performance
  • Lead the ecommerce side of new product launches, including landing pages, pre-order flows, launch-day experience, and post-launch optimization.

Subscription Growth & Monetization

  • Evolve subscription strategy end-to-end: conversion, pricing, packaging, and plan tiers, with a clear point of view on how each lever ladders up to LTV
  • Identify and prioritize opportunities for upsell, cross-sell, and revenue expansion within the existing subscriber base

Advocacy and Word-of-Mouth

  • Develop product-led growth initiatives that turn Nanit’s engaged parent community into scalable acquisition and retention engine through referrals, reviews, UGC, gifting, and community driven experiences

Cross-Functional Leadership

  • Define and track KPIs across the full funnel: site traffic, conversion rate, AOV, subscription attach rate, churn, retention, and LTV
  • Translate customer insights, experimentation, and data into actionable roadmaps.
  • Collaborate cross functionally with engineering, design, data, marketing, and customer success to deliver high-impact customer experiences and timely product delivery
  • Stay current on trends in DTC ecommerce, subscriptions, and connected device monetization.
  • Communicate product updates, performance, and strategic recommendations to stakeholders and senior leadership.

Who You Are:

  • 5+ yrs of product management experience, in ecommerce/DTC and/or subscription product companies
  • Experience at a consumer hardware, connected device, or hardware + software/subscription company is strongly preferred (e.g., baby tech, wearables, connected home, health/wellness devices).
  • Deep data fluency: comfortable with analytics tools (building dashboards, running experiments, and translating data into strategy.
  • Experience with Shopify and the modern DTC stack (e.g., Klaviyo, Attentive, Yotpo, Recharge, Cordial, FiveTran).
  • Strong cross-functional collaborator - effective at working with marketing, growth, engineering, design, ops, and customer success, especially in areas with shared ownership
  • Excellent prioritization instincts. Comfortable managing a broad scope with lean resources and making smart tradeoffs for maximum impact.
  • Clear communicator - equally comfortable presenting a roadmap to leadership and writing detailed user stories for engineering.

Why You’ll Love Working Here:

  • Hybrid in office schedule
  • Remote work from home month in August
  • Flexible PTO (we trust you to take the time you need)
  • Equity options so you can share in our growth
  • Paid parental leave for all new parents
  • Employee discounts on Nanit products
  • Work from home stipend
  • Monthly team events

EEO, Salary and Location:

This is a hybrid role that will require prospective candidates to be in our NYC office up to 3 days per week.

Salary Range: $180,000 to $200,000 base salary + plus equity and benefits. The base pay is one component of Nanit’s total compensation package, which may also include access to healthcare benefits, a 401(k) plan, short-term and long-term disability coverage, and basic life insurance. Ultimately, in determining your pay, we’ll consider your location, experience, and other job-related factors.

We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected class.

jobs.title: Software Engineering Manager
jobs.description:
About Turing

Based in San Francisco, California, Turing is the world’s leading research accelerator for frontier AI labs and a trusted partner for global enterprises looking to deploy advanced AI systems. Turing accelerates frontier research with high-quality data, specialized talent, and training pipelines that advance thinking, reasoning, coding, multimodality, and STEM. For enterprises, Turing builds proprietary intelligence systems that integrate AI into mission-critical workflows, unlock transformative outcomes, and drive lasting competitive advantage.

Recognized by Forbes, The Information, and Fast Company among the world’s top innovators, Turing’s leadership team includes AI technologists from Meta, Google, Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, McKinsey, Bain, Stanford, Caltech, and MIT. Learn more at www.turing.com

About the Role

We’re hiring an Engineering Manager to lead and grow a team of engineers while staying close to the technology. You’ll own your team’s delivery, health, and growth — hiring and developing engineers, driving execution, and partnering across functions — without losing the technical judgment that earns your team’s trust. This is a people-leadership role for someone who has built and run high-performing teams and brings a strong engineering foundation to the job.

What You’ll Do

  • Own your team’s delivery, health, and growth — planning, execution, and shipping across one or more teams.
  • Hire, grow, and retain engineers; run 1:1s, performance management, and career development.
  • Set and raise engineering standards, processes, and ways of working.
  • Provide technical direction and pressure-test decisions without micromanaging.
  • Partner with product, design, and leadership; manage up and across.
  • Own team-level operational excellence, including incident and on-call ownership.

What We’re Looking For

  • 8–12 years of total engineering experience, including 3+ years of direct engineering leadership (people management) — owning a team’s delivery, growth, and performance, not solely tech-lead/IC seniority.
  • A track record of hiring, growing, and retaining engineers, with positive retention and team-health outcomes.
  • Delivery ownership across one or more teams and strong cross-functional partnership with PM, design, and leadership.
  • A strong underlying engineering foundation comparable to a Staff Engineer: driving architecture and technical direction across multiple services or teams.
  • Deep distributed systems and cloud-native architecture knowledge; engineering judgment retained alongside the management role.
  • Demonstrated impact on multi-team technical direction, migrations/modernization, reliability/performance, and engineering standards.
  • A degree in Computer Science, IT, or a related engineering field is preferred but not required — we weight work outcomes most heavily.

Nice to Have

  • AI platform architecture experience.
  • Agentic systems / LLM infrastructure experience.

Values

  • We are client first: We put our clients at the center of everything we do, because their success is the ultimate measure of our value.

  • We work at Start-Up Speed: We move fast, stay agile and favor action because momentum is the foundation of perfection

  • We are AI forward: We help our clients build the future of Al and implement it in our own roles and workflow to amplify productivity.

Advantages of joining Turing

  • Amazing work culture (Super collaborative & supportive work environment; 5 days a week)

  • Awesome colleagues (Surround yourself with top talent from Meta, Google, LinkedIn etc. as well as people with deep startup experience)

  • Competitive compensation

  • Flexible working hours

Don’t meet every single requirement? Studies have shown that women and people of color are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. Turing is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability, protected veteran status, or any other legally protected characteristics. At Turing we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace  and celebrate authenticity, so if you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyways. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

For applicants from the European Union, please review Turing’s GDPR notice here.

jobs.title: Strategic Client Executive, Public Sector
jobs.description:
InterSystems Public Sector Corporation (IPSC) is seeking a Strategic Client Executive to join its State Government sales organization. This role is designed for professionals with experience in state government, public policy, legislative affairs, political campaigns, government relations, healthcare administration, or related fields who are interested in building a long-term fulfilling career in enterprise technology sales.

Working alongside experienced professionals, Sales Engineers, and executive leadership, the Strategic Client Executive will learn how to identify opportunities, develop executive relationships, and position InterSystems’ technology solutions to address the healthcare modernization priorities of state governments.

This position is ideal for individuals who understand how state government works and possess strong communication, relationship-building, and strategic thinking skills but may have limited enterprise software sales experience.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Develop and execute account strategies across assigned state government accounts.
  • Research legislative, regulatory, and funding initiatives to identify emerging business opportunities.
  • Build relationships with state agency staff, legislative offices, governors’ offices, healthcare associations, and other key stakeholders.
  • Attend customer meetings, conferences, association events, and executive briefings to increase InterSystems’ visibility within assigned territories.
  • Assist in developing account plans, stakeholder maps, opportunity strategies, and executive engagement plans.
  • Coordinate internal resources to support customer pursuits, including Sales Engineering, Marketing, Product Management, and Executive Leadership.
  • Learn or advance knowledge in state procurement processes, budgeting cycles, and public-sector funding mechanisms.
  • Support proposal development, customer presentations, and business development campaigns.
  • Maintain accurate customer information and opportunity tracking within tracking system.
  • Participate in a structured professional development program.

Requirements

  • 2+ years of professional experience in state government, executive branch, legislature, public policy, political campaigns, government affairs, business development, consulting, or a related field.
  • Demonstrated understanding of state government operations, political and policy environments, and the roles of executive agencies, legislative bodies, and key public-sector stakeholders.
  • Strong relationship-building, written communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage professionally with senior leaders and external stakeholders.
  • Demonstrated interest in developing a career in enterprise technology sales and a willingness to complete structured sales, healthcare industry, and InterSystems technology training.
  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent combination of education and relevant professional experience.

Preferred Background

Candidates may come from backgrounds such as:

  • Governor’s Office
  • State Legislature
  • State Agency
  • Medicaid Agency
  • Public Health
  • Legislative Caucus
  • Congressional or State Campaigns
  • Government Affairs
  • Public Policy
  • Healthcare Association
  • Consulting
  • Military or Veterans Affairs
  • Local Government

Enterprise technology sales experience is welcomed but not required. We are seeking individuals who possess intellectual curiosity, executive presence, strong communication skills, and a passion for improving government through technology.

InterSystems Public Sector actively recruits talented professionals. All recruiting activities are conducted in accordance with applicable ethics laws, revolving-door restrictions, lobbying regulations, procurement rules, and employer policies. Candidates are expected to disclose any applicable post-employment restrictions, and InterSystems will work with employees to ensure compliance with all legal and ethical obligations before they engage with former agencies or matters in which they previously participated.

We are an equal-opportunity employer and do not discriminate because of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, ancestry, marital status, veteran status, age, disability, sexual orientation or gender identity or expression or any other legally protected category. InterSystems is an E-Verify Employer in the United States.

About InterSystems

InterSystems, a creative data technology provider, delivers a unified foundation for next-generation applications for healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and supply chain customers in more than 80 countries. Our data platforms solve interoperability, speed, and scalability problems for large organizations around the globe to unlock the power of data and allow people to perceive data in imaginative ways. Established in 1978, InterSystems is committed to excellence through its 24×7 support for customers and partners around the world. Privately held and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, InterSystems has 38 offices in 28 countries worldwide. For more information, please visit InterSystems.com.

AI Disclaimer

InterSystems may use AI tools for its internal operations including administrative tasks during recruitment (e.g., organizing candidate information).  InterSystems’ approach to AI is guided by the InterSystems Responsible AI Guidelines. AI is not used to make or influence hiring decisions. All decisions are made by InterSystems employees.

Candidates may use AI for CV or interview preparation, provided materials are truthful and reflect their own experience.  AI tools and third-party transcription services must not be used during interviews or assessments.

jobs.title: AI Specialist - Enterprise & Strategic Accounts, AgentControl
jobs.description:
About the Job:

LaunchDarkly just launched AgentControl — the operational layer that lets teams configure, evaluate, and control AI agents at runtime, no redeploy required. We’re looking for an AI Specialist to join our Enterprise & Strategic sales team as a quota-carrying overlay seller, driving AgentControl revenue across our largest accounts.

This is a sales-led role, not a technical pre-sales seat. You’ll own an AgentControl number, build your own pipeline alongside Enterprise and Strategic Account Executives, and run deals start to close.

Responsibilities:

  • Own a revenue target for AgentControl, with full accountability for pipeline, forecasting, and closed-won bookings
  • Partner with Enterprise and Strategic AEs as the AgentControl overlay — building account plans and co-selling into existing and net-new relationships
  • Prospect within the install base and target accounts to create AgentControl opportunities, not just work what’s handed to you
  • Run the full sales cycle: discovery, value narrative, stakeholder alignment, negotiation, and close
  • Build a business case for economic buyers around AgentControl’s core value — safer, faster AI agent operations in production
  • Partner with Deal Desk on pricing and deal structure for large or complex deals
  • Work cross-functionally with Sales Engineering and Product Marketing on positioning, and pull in technical support at the right moments
  • Lead executive conversations and proof-of-value engagements that move strategic deals forward
  • Feed field intelligence — win/loss themes, objections, pricing feedback — back to Product and Marketing

Qualifications:

  • 5+ years of quota-carrying sales experience — AE, sales specialist, or overlay seller — ideally in enterprise/strategic B2B SaaS
  • A proven track record of hitting or exceeding revenue targets
  • Experience running full-cycle enterprise deals: prospecting, discovery, business case, negotiation, close
  • Comfort operating as an overlay — co-selling with AEs without owning the whole account relationship
  • Working familiarity with modern software delivery (CI/CD, feature management, or observability) and genuine interest in AI agents in production
  • Strong commercial instincts and executive presence — equally at home prospecting a new logo or briefing a VP of Engineering
  • Experience ramping a new product line within an existing territory is a plus
  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience

Pay:

Target pay ranges based on Geographic Zones* for Level 4:

  • Zone 1: San Francisco/Bay Area or NYC Metropolitan Area, Boston, Seattle - _$2 69,000 - $370,000_**
  • Zone 2: Irvine, LA, Monterey, Santa Barbara, Santa Rosa, Austin, Portland, Philadelphia, Chicago - $242,000 - $333,000**
  • Zone 3: All other US locations - $229,000 - $314,000**

LaunchDarkly operates from a place of high trust and transparency; we are happy to state the pay range for our open roles to best align with your needs. Exact compensation may vary based on skills, experience, and location.

*Within the United States, our geographic pay zones are defined by counties surrounding major metropolitan areas.

**Restricted Stock Units (RSUs), health, vision, and dental insurance, and mental health benefits in addition to salary.

About LaunchDarkly:

Modern software delivery was supposed to be the foundation for a thriving digital business but reality has proven otherwise. Slow, inefficient development cycles, costly outages, and fragmented customer experiences are preventing developers from building their best software. The LaunchDarkly platform helps developers innovate on new features faster while protecting them with a safety valve to instantly rewind when things go wrong. Developers can target product experiences to any customer segment and maximize the business impact of every feature. And by gradually rolling out new application components, they escape nightmare “big-bang” technology migrations.

The LaunchDarkly platform was built to guide engineers to the next frontier of DevOps by:

  • Improving the velocity and stability of software releases, without the fear of end customer outages
  • Delivering targeted experiences by easily personalizing features to customer cohorts
  • Maximizing the business impact of every feature through the ability to experiment and optimize
  • Coordinating the release and optimization of software to provide consistent experiences across mobile platforms and device types
  • Improving the effectiveness and productivity of engineering teams, by providing insights into engineering cadence and stability

At LaunchDarkly, we believe in the power of teams. We’re building a team that is humble, open, collaborative, respectful and kind. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. LD invites any applicant to review our written Affirmative Action Plan. To do so, contact People Ops at hr@launchdarkly.com.

Do you need a disability accommodation?

Fill out this accommodations request form and someone from our People Operations team will contact you for assistance.

Your safety matters to us. To protect yourself from potential scams, LaunchDarkly recruiters will only contact you from @LaunchDarkly.com email addresses or via LinkedIn from “Verified Recruiter” accounts.Be cautious of emails from other domains.  Legitimate LaunchDarkly recruiters will never ask for money, fees, or banking information before making a job offer. LaunchDarkly will never make a job offer without conducting a formal interview process. Our interview process does not involve asking detailed questions by email. If you are ever unsure about a communication that you receive, don’t click any links—visit Careers | LaunchDarkly  directly for confirmed job openings and links to apply.

Please notify us of any fraudulent representation by sending an email to careers@launchdarkly.com.

jobs.title: Software Engineering Manager - Select by DoiT
jobs.description:
The Opportunity

As an Engineering Manager, you will lead and grow a team responsible for building and scaling SELECT, DoiT’s cloud cost management platform. You’ll play a critical role in shaping the product roadmap, technical strategy and the growth of the engineering organization.

This is a hands-on technical leadership role where you’ll partner first and foremost with our engineers, and with product, design, and executive leadership to drive execution, improve engineering effectiveness, and deliver meaningful outcomes for customers. While this is not an individual contributor role, we’re looking for a leader with a history of product and engineering excellence who can confidently set technical direction, scrutinise and guide engineering designs and decisions, and develop our engineers.

You’ll join a highly collaborative, fast-moving team operating with startup agility while benefiting from the resources and scale of DoiT. As SELECT continues to grow following its acquisition by DoiT, you’ll have a significant opportunity to set the direction of the product, team, and engineering culture.

Responsibilities

Technical Leadership

  • Partner with engineers to design scalable, reliable, and maintainable products across our application, data platform, and infrastructure. We use scalable to reflect both performance and also from a product cohesion and expansion perspective.
  • Lead technical discussions and architectural reviews, helping teams make thoughtful trade-offs between speed, quality, scalability, and customer impact.
  • Drive engineering excellence through strong development practices, code quality standards, observability, reliability, and operational maturity.
  • Provide technical guidance across technologies including Python, FastAPI, Postgres, dbt, Dagster, Snowflake, and cloud-native services.
  • Ensure decisions consciously evaluate short term and long term considerations of speed, maintainability, and growth.

Team Leadership & People Management

  • Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing team of engineers through coaching, feedback, and career development.
  • Create an environment that promotes accountability, ownership, collaboration, and continuous learning.
  • Conduct performance management, goal setting, growth planning, and regular one-on-one meetings.
  • Partner with Talent Acquisition and leadership to attract, interview, and hire exceptional engineering talent.
  • Help scale the organization while maintaining a strong engineering culture and high hiring bar.

Execution & Cross-Functional Leadership

  • Partner closely with Product and Design to align technical execution with business priorities and customer needs.
  • Drive planning, prioritization, and delivery across multiple initiatives while balancing technical investments and feature development.
  • Identify risks, dependencies, and operational bottlenecks early and proactively drive resolution.
  • Foster strong communication across engineering, product, sales, and leadership.
  • Establish and improve engineering processes that increase team effectiveness and delivery predictability.

Operational Excellence

  • Own team health, and operational performance.
  • Drive improvements in reliability, performance, incident response, and service ownership.
  • Champion a culture of continuous improvement through retrospectives, measurement, and experimentation.
  • Ensure teams maintain a high standard of documentation, testing, and technical decision-making.

Qualifications

  • 5+ years of software engineering experience, including experience building and operating production systems.
  • 2+ years of engineering leadership or management experience leading software engineering teams.
  • Strong technical background in backend engineering, data modelling and data-intensive systems.
  • Experience working with technologies such as Python, FastAPI, Postgres, dbt, Dagster, Snowflake, or similar modern data platforms.
  • Demonstrated ability to lead architectural discussions and influence technical direction without being the primary individual contributor.
  • Experience partnering with Product and cross-functional stakeholders to deliver customer-focused outcomes.
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to engage engineers, executives, and business stakeholders.
  • Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, ambiguous, and highly collaborative environment.

Bonus Points

  • Experience building SaaS products in startup or high-growth environments.
  • Experience with FinOps, cloud cost management, cloud infrastructure, or data platform products.
  • Experience leading distributed remote engineering teams.
  • Experience scaling teams and processes following periods of rapid growth or acquisition.

What Success Looks Like

In your first year, you will:

  • Support, maintain and grow engagement and performance in the engineering team.
  • Increase delivery velocity while maintaining technical quality.
  • Establish strong cross-functional partnerships across Product, Design, and Leadership.
  • Shape the roadmap and architecture of the SELECT platform.
  • Raise the bar for engineering execution, operational excellence, and team development.
  • Play a key role in scaling both the product and the organization as SELECT continues its next phase of growth within DoiT.

jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer,  API Platform
jobs.description:
About Nayya

Founded in 2019, Nayya is on a mission to connect people’s most important information, so they can thrive in their health and wealth. Powered by AI and advanced analytics, Nayya’s platform transforms complex benefits experiences into intuitive, seamless, and ongoing interactions—meeting people’s real world needs. As a trusted platform and partner to leading employers, benefits solutions, and HR tech providers, Nayya unlocks long-term value through helping employees live more resilient lives. Backed by strategic investors like ICONIQ, Felicis Ventures, SemperVirens, Workday Ventures, MetLife Nextgen Ventures, and ADP Ventures, Nayya is ushering in the future of health and wealth for all.

About the team

The API Platform team builds the foundation everything else at Nayya runs on, the APIs, services, and developer tools that power our products and our partner integrations. When a carrier sends us data, an HR platform calls a Nayya endpoint, or MCP tool via it’s agent it goes through our platform.

We’re now building toward a future where AI agents are first-class consumers of our platform. That means designing APIs, tools, and protocols that agents and MCP servers ours and our partners’ can use safely, predictably, and at scale to act on behalf of members. If you’re excited about shaping what an “agent-native” benefits platform looks like, this is the team to do it on.

What you’ll do

  • Design, build, and operate the APIs and services that power Nayya’s products, partner integrations, and AI agents
  • Make our platform genuinely great for agents to consume, clear schemas, predictable behavior, strong guardrails, idempotency, and the observability needed to debug non-deterministic callers
  • Define Nayya’s agent-facing infrastructure: tool definitions, MCP-style protocols, agent authentication and authorization, action auditing, and safety controls
  • Own services end-to-end, from architecture through deployment, monitoring, and on-call
  • Improve the developer experience for internal teams, external partners, and the AI engineers building on top of the platform
  • Set technical direction for the API Platform: API standards, versioning, authentication, rate limiting, and observability
  • Mentor engineers across the company, raise the bar on design reviews, and help shape engineering culture
  • Partner closely with security and infrastructure to keep us fast, reliable, and HIPAA-compliant

What we’re looking for

  • 6+ years of professional software engineering experience, with significant time building backend services and public or partner-facing APIs
  • Deep expertise in API design (REST, GraphQL, or gRPC), distributed systems, and the tradeoffs that come with both
  • Strong production experience with any of Python / Go / Node / Typescript / Ruby on Rails on AWS.
  • A point of view on what makes an API or tool good (or bad) for an AI agent to use, and the instincts to design for that from day one
  • Track record of leading projects that span multiple teams, and mentoring the engineers around you
  • Comfortable owning operational quality observability, on-call, incident response, and continuous improvement
  • Excellent written and verbal communication; you can explain a technical decision to engineers, PMs, and partners equally well

Nice to have

  • Hands-on experience building with LLMs — function/tool calling, prompt and context design, evals, and the patterns that make AI systems work reliably in production
  • Experience designing or shipping agent-facing infrastructure — MCP servers, tool catalogs, agent gateways, or similar
  • Familiarity with agent frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, LangGraph, etc.) and evaluation tooling for LLM applications
  • Experience with authentication and authorization patterns for agents acting on behalf of users (delegated auth, scoped tokens, action approval flows)
  • Experience in healthtech, fintech, insurance, or other regulated industries
  • Working knowledge of HIPAA, SOC 2, or similar compliance frameworks
  • Open-source contributions or public technical writing

Why Nayya

  • Direct impact on a problem that touches almost every working American
  • A team that takes ownership seriously and ships
  • Competitive compensation, equity, and a benefits package worthy of a benefits company

The salary range for New York based candidates for this role is $160,000- $200,000. We use a location factor to adjust this range for candidates that are located outside of geographic region of our New York office. Placement within the salary band is determined based on experience.

Nayya is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics

jobs.title: Senior Frontend Engineer II - Europe
jobs.description:
Storyblok is a headless CMS that enables marketers and developers to create with joy and succeed in the AI-driven content era. It empowers you to deliver structured and consistent content everywhere: websites, apps, AI search, and beyond.

Marketers get a visual editor with reusable components, in-context preview, and workflows to launch fast and stay on brand. Developers have freedom to use their favorite frameworks and integrate with anything through the API-first platform. Brands get one source of truth for content that is accurate, flexible, and measurable.

Legendary brands like Virgin Media O2, Oatly, and TomTom use Storyblok to make a bigger, faster market impact. It’s Joyful Headless™, and it changes everything.

WHAT IS IN IT FOR YOU

You will be joining a growing company where you can contribute to many “firsts”. Plus these benefits:

  • Monthly remote work stipend (home internet costs, electricity). Home office equipment package right at the start (laptop, keyboard, monitor…)
  • Home office equipment upgrade (furniture, ear plugs …) or membership to a local co-working space after your onboarding
  • Sick leave benefit, parental leave and 25 days of annual leave plus your local national holidays
  • Personal development fund for courses, books, conferences, and material
  • VSOP (Virtual Stock Option Plan)
  • The annual international team-building trip, quarterly and monthly online get-togethers
  • As a fully remote company, with work-life balance at its core, you’ll enjoy flexible schedules
  • An international team that loves to have fun at work and works hard together to accomplish shared goals

JOB SUMMARY

We are looking for a Senior Frontend Engineer highly skilled with JavaScript (TypeScript) and at least one frontend framework to join our Product Engineering team at Storyblok. In this role, you’ll go beyond feature delivery: you’ll drive domain-level improvements, lead complex technical initiatives, and act as a key reference point for frontend engineering decisions across multiple projects. You will have technical ownership of a frontend domain and help shape how we build and scale our product.

You’ll work closely with engineers, product managers, and other stakeholders to break down ambiguous problems, define technically sound approaches, and raise engineering standards across your domain. If you’re someone who cares deeply about code quality, scalable architecture, and helping those around you grow, this role is a great fit.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS

  • Ship marketable features for Storyblok app using modern web technologies (e.g TypeScript, Vue, Vite, etc).

  • Own the end-to-end Software Development Lifecycle, from reviewing product requirements to deliver the software reliably to customers across disciplines.

  • Take technical responsibility for a frontend subsystem (domain), ensuring long-term maintainability, scalability, and reliability.

  • Identify systemic risks and architectural bottlenecks within your domain and drive remediation proactively.

  • Break down ambiguous technical problems into clear strategies and executable implementation plans.

  • Raise engineering standards through thorough code reviews, documentation, and technical guidance.

  • Write technical design documents for domain-level initiatives and communicate trade-offs clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

  • Contribute hands-on in critical areas where technical depth is required, including refactoring, performance improvements, and security considerations.

  • Mentor engineers across projects, helping grow technical ownership and capability within the team.

  • Help Engineering Managers make informed technical decisions by providing clear context and recommendations.

  • Identify and address long-term technical improvements proactively, balancing short-term delivery with system sustainability.

EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE

  • A degree in Computer Science or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
  • At least 6+ years of relevant experience as a Frontend Engineer.
  • Proficiency in modern frontend technologies (e.g., JavaScript, TypeScript, Vue.js, React, or similar frameworks).
  • Thorough knowledge about JavaScript/Typescript languages and their modern syntax and features.
  • Solid understanding of web fundamentals: HTML, CSS, browser APIs, performance, security and accessibility considerations.
  • Comprehensive knowledge of software testing concepts (testability, regression, unit, integration, test automation) and testing frameworks e.g. Cypress and Playwright.
  • Familiarity with CI/CD workflows and the ability to monitor test pipelines and escalate issues.
